title: Documentation incorrectly claims grep/glob respect .gitignore – they use rg --glob which overrides ignore files
author: YUyuchao
body:
### Description

The [official documentation](https://opencode.ai/docs/tools/#internal-mechanisms) states:

"Internally, tools like grep and glob use [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep). By default, ripgrep respects patterns in .gitignore, meaning files and directories listed in .gitignore will be excluded from search and listing results."

This claim is factually incorrect and misleading. glob invoke ripgrep with the --glob flag, which overrides all other ignore logic (including .gitignore, .ignore, and .rgignore) as clearly stated in rg --help:

-g GLOB, --glob=GLOB — Include or exclude files and directories for searching that match the given glob. This always overrides any other ignore logic.

Consequently, files that should be excluded are included in search results, causing unnecessary noise and confusion.

This issue is particularly concerning because the documentation directly contradicts the actual behavior, leading users to wrongly believe their ignore files are respected.

### Plugins

none

### OpenCode version

1.17.20

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a project with a .ignore file excluding dist/ and *.log.
2. Ask to glob

### Screenshot and/or share link

https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep/issues/1808

### Operating System

Windows11

### Terminal

powershell

title: Character-level changes are difficult to identify in side-by-side diff review
author: jabaruben
body: ### Description When OpenCode asks for approval before applying changes, the side-by-side diff highlights the entire modified line but does not clearly highlight the exact characters that changed. This makes some modifications surprisingly difficult to review, especially when only one or a few characters are different. For example, I recently had a change where the only difference was a single character in a file path (`B` → `P`). Although the line was marked as modified, I had to compare both lines character by character before noticing the actual change. The attached screenshot shows another example where entire lines are highlighted, but the specific additions, removals, and replacements inside those lines are not immediately distinguishable. ### Plugins _No response_ ### OpenCode version 1.17.18 ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open a project in OpenCode. 2. Ask the agent to modify an existing file. 3. Wait until the approval dialog with the side-by-side diff appears. 4. Review a change where only a few characters differ inside an otherwise similar line. 5. Notice that the changed characters are difficult to identify without manually comparing the two lines character by character. ### Screenshot and/or share link ### Operating System Windows 10 ### Terminal cmd

title: Composer can desync after switching back to Desktop in new layout
author: AidenGeunGeun
body:
### Description

With "New layout and designs" enabled in Desktop, switching away with Cmd+Tab and returning can intermittently leave the composer unusable. Typed text appears on top of the placeholder, pressing Space clears it, and Enter does not submit. Switching to another OpenCode tab and back resets the composer.

In the captured failure state, the contenteditable DOM contains text while the prompt state is still empty. An active composition then prevents Enter and allows the empty prompt state to overwrite the visible text.

Expected: returning to Desktop preserves the composer text, hides the placeholder, and leaves Space and Enter working normally.

Related fixes #8275 and #17041 cover stale IME state and editor reconciliation, but neither handles this window-focus transition.

### Plugins

None

### OpenCode version

1.17.19 observed; failure state reproduced on 1.17.20 and current `dev`

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable "New layout and designs" in OpenCode Desktop.
2. Focus the composer and begin typing.
3. Cmd+Tab to another app, then return to the existing OpenCode window.
4. Continue typing and try Space and Enter.
5. If necessary, repeat; the native focus transition is intermittent.

### Screenshot and/or share link

The failure state shows typed text rendered over the placeholder. A deterministic regression and before/after evidence will be included with the fix.

### Operating System

macOS 26.6 arm64

### Terminal

Ghostty

title: Context usage tooltip disappears while still hovered in new layout
author: AidenGeunGeun
body:
### Description

With "New layout and designs" enabled, the context usage tooltip in the sticky session header closes as soon as the timeline scrolls, even though the pointer is still over the ring. This also happens during streaming auto-scroll, and the tooltip stays closed until the pointer leaves and re-enters.

The trigger is inside the timeline scroller, while the tooltip library dismisses an open tooltip when a containing element scrolls. Since this trigger stays pinned and hovered, that dismissal is unexpected.

Expected: the tooltip remains visible while the ring is hovered. Moving the pointer away or pressing Escape should still close it.

I reproduced this in the official 1.17.20 macOS arm64 app. The same path is unchanged on `dev` at `cb8be9ba1217c2e7a2b93cf513eb21b41a7f5365`.

### Plugins

None

### OpenCode version

1.17.20

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable "New layout and designs".
2. Open a session with a scrollable timeline.
3. Hover the context usage ring in the top-right header.
4. Keep the pointer over the ring and scroll the timeline, or let streaming output auto-scroll it.
5. The tooltip disappears and does not return until the pointer leaves and re-enters.

### Screenshot and/or share link

A short before/after recording will be included with the fix.

### Operating System

macOS 26.6 arm64

### Terminal

Ghostty

title: Desktop: Terminal button missing for months - plea for priority fix

## Problem

The terminal toggle button has been missing from the Desktop app titlebar since v1.15.6. This has been reported in multiple issues:

- #29829 (May 2026)
- #29951 (May 2026)  
- #31878 (June 2026)

This is a basic UI feature that many users rely on. The workaround (disabling New Layout Designs) is not ideal and should not be necessary months after the regression was introduced.

## Request

Please prioritize merging the fix (PR #32599) or provide an official timeline for resolution. This has been dragging on for too long.

- OpenCode Desktop v1.17.3
- macOS

Thank you.

title: Skill tool completely broken
author: yyy1121
body: ### Description **OpenCode Bug Report — skill tool completely broken** --- ### Environment - **OpenCode version**: 1.17.20 (macOS Desktop App) - **OS**: macOS (Darwin) - **Install path**: `/Applications/OpenCode.app` --- ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open OpenCode Desktop 2. In any session, call `skill({ name: "excel-automation" })` or any skill (including built-in `customize-opencode`) 3. Observe the return value --- ### Expected Behavior The `skill` tool loads and injects the SKILL.md content into context. --- ### Actual Behavior **All** skill invocations (7 external + 1 built-in) return: ``` All fibers interrupted without error ``` No stack trace, no error code, no permission prompt. --- ### Troubleshooting Done (all ineffective) | Check | Status | |-------|--------| | Skill name uniqueness | ✅ Only `~/.agents/skills/` remains as source | | SKILL.md frontmatter validity | ✅ `name`, `description`, `license` all compliant | | Permissions | ✅ Default `allow`; log shows `permission=skill action=allow` | | Cache clear | ✅ `rm -rf ~/.cache/opencode ~/.local/share/opencode` | | Full app restart | ✅ Multiple times | | Version upgrade | ✅ 1.17.18 → 1.17.20 | --- ### Key Log Excerpt ``` # ~/.local/share/opencode/log/opencode.log timestamp=... level=INFO message="evaluated permission=skill pattern=excel-automation action.permission=* action.action=allow" # No subsequent error/warn — tool silently aborts ``` Recurring at startup (may be related): ``` background dependency install failed dir=/Users/wby/.config/opencode error="@opencode-ai/plugin: No matching version found for @opencode-ai/plugin@local." ``` --- ### Severity **Critical** — entire skill system unusable, blocks all skill-dependent workflows. ### Plugins _No response_ ### OpenCode version 1.17.20 ### Steps to reproduce Open OpenCode Desktop In any session, call skill({ name: "excel-automation" }) or any skill (including built-in customize-opencode) Observe the return value ### Screenshot and/or share link ### Operating System MacOS 27.0 Beta(26A5378n) ### Terminal _No response_

title: Performance Regression: Extremely Slow "Thinking" Phase After Updating to v1.17.20
author: CyberNiteshHub
body:
### Description

## Summary

After updating OpenCode Desktop to **v1.17.20** on **14 July 2026**, I noticed a significant performance regression.

The application itself opens normally, but the **Thinking** phase has become extremely slow. Even simple prompts that previously received responses almost instantly now spend a long time in the "Thinking" state before generating any output.

This issue started immediately after updating to **v1.17.20**.

---

## Environment

- **OpenCode Version:** v1.17.20
- **Operating System:** Windows 11
- **Installation:** Official OpenCode Desktop application

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Update OpenCode Desktop to **v1.17.20**.
2. Open any project.
3. Ask a simple question or request.
4. Observe the **Thinking** phase.

---

## Expected Behavior

The Thinking phase should complete quickly, similar to previous versions.

---

## Actual Behavior

The Thinking phase takes significantly longer than before, even for simple prompts. The delay happens before any response starts generating, making the application feel much slower overall.

The issue occurs across different projects and is not limited to a specific workspace.

---

## Additional Notes

Before updating to **v1.17.20**, OpenCode responded much faster under the same conditions. The slowdown only appeared after installing this update.

If needed, I can provide logs or additional information to help diagnose the issue.

Thank you for your work on OpenCode!

title: Feature Request: Native Visual Image Understanding for UI & Design Analysis
author: CyberNiteshHub
body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Hi OpenCode team,

I would love to see native image understanding support in OpenCode.

Currently, images can be uploaded, but OpenCode mainly extracts text or document content. It cannot reliably understand visual elements such as UI layouts, dashboards, website designs, color schemes, spacing, icons, or component structures.

It would be extremely useful if OpenCode could analyze images the way modern multimodal AI assistants do, allowing users to:

Understand UI/UX designs and layouts
Recreate interfaces from screenshots
Analyze design structure, spacing, and components
Generate code based on a design image
Suggest improvements to existing designs

This would make OpenCode much more powerful for frontend development and design-to-code workflows.

Thank you for considering this feature.

title: [FEATURE]: Extract transcript persistence projection
author: HOYALIM
body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Extract the database-backed transcript persistence adapter from `packages/core/src/session/projector.ts` into `packages/core/src/session/projection/transcript.ts`.

This is the first reviewable slice explicitly specified by #36473. It should move message schema encoding/decoding, session-scoped assistant/shell/running-compaction lookups, message updates, and durable-sequence insertion without changing SQL queries, transaction ownership, or projector registration order.

The existing projector integration tests should continue to exercise the behavior through `events.publish`. EventV2 typing changes and the later fork, transition, usage, and V1 projection extractions are out of scope.

Acceptance criteria:

- `projector.ts` delegates transcript events and promoted-message insertion to the extracted module.
- Existing session projector integration tests pass unchanged.
- The extraction introduces no transaction, query, schema, migration, or registration-order changes.

title: opencode.ai resolves to four IPv6 addresses and stalls Bun fetch on IPv6 blackhole networks
author: heimoshuiyu
body:
### Description

Over the past two days, multiple OpenCode users in China have reported that `opencode-go` becomes stuck indefinitely on networks with broken or blackholed IPv6. Other applications, including browsers accessing `https://opencode.ai`, quickly fall back to IPv4 and work normally.

`opencode.ai` returns exactly four AAAA records, while Bun/uSockets uses four concurrent connection slots. My hypothesis is that the four unreachable IPv6 addresses occupy every slot and block IPv4 fallback.

I reproduced this on dual-stack AWS by dropping TCP/443 only for the four IPv6 addresses while keeping IPv4 healthy. Bun 1.3.14 took 132 seconds to fall back to IPv4, and OpenCode v1.17.20 took approximately 133 seconds to receive an HTTP response. The test used the same OpenCode Go endpoint with dummy credentials because the delay occurs before authentication.

Could the number of AAAA records returned by `opencode.ai` be reduced from four to two as an immediate mitigation?

Related reports: #36029 and #35035. Related Bun issue: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/33278

### Plugins

None

### OpenCode version

1.17.20 & v2 latest commit

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use a dual-stack machine with working public IPv4 and IPv6.

2. Confirm that `opencode.ai` returns four A and four AAAA records:

   ```bash
   dig @1.1.1.1 opencode.ai A +short
   dig @1.1.1.1 opencode.ai AAAA +short
   ```

3. Silently drop TCP/443 traffic to the four IPv6 addresses while leaving IPv4 working:

   ```bash
   sudo nft add table inet opencode_repro
   sudo nft 'add chain inet opencode_repro output { type filter hook output priority -100; policy accept; }'
   sudo nft 'add rule inet opencode_repro output ip6 daddr { 2606:4700:78::90:0:140, 2606:4700:78::90:0:141, 2606:4700:78::90:0:142, 2606:4700:78::90:0:143 } tcp dport 443 counter drop'
   ```

4. Verify that IPv4 works while IPv6 times out:

   ```bash
   curl -4 --connect-timeout 5 https://opencode.ai/
   curl -6 --connect-timeout 5 https://opencode.ai/
   ```

5. Run OpenCode Go using an account with `opencode-go` configured:

   ```bash
   time opencode run --model opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash "Reply with hi"
   ```

6. The request should stall while the four IPv6 connections are blackholed. Compare with IPv6 disabled for the process:

   ```bash
   time BUN_FEATURE_FLAG_DISABLE_IPV6=1 opencode run --model opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash "Reply with hi"
   ```

### Screenshot and/or share link

N/A. Reproduced with the commands above.

### Operating System

Amazon Linux 2023, kernel 6.1.176-221.360.amzn2023.x86_64

### Terminal

Not relevant; reproduced with non-interactive `opencode run`

title: Serve mode: multi-tool-call turn silently drops one bash dispatch; session stuck "busy" forever (deterministic repro: 10/10 on v1.17.18 and v1.17.20)
author: Smerked
body:
### Description

In opencode serve mode, when the model emits two bash tool calls in a single response, one call executes and completes normally, while the other is never dispatched at all — no process is spawned, no filesystem effect occurs — yet its part state remains "status": "running" indefinitely. The session then reports {"type":"busy"} forever. POST /session/{id}/abort is the only recovery.

Key discriminator vs. similar reports: the dropped command was mkdir -p <path> and the directory was never created. This is not "tool completed but session failed to resume" (#22156) — the dispatch itself is lost. The sibling call from the same turn completes fine (17ms, normal exit).

Environment

  • opencode v1.17.18 and v1.17.20 (opencode serve), both reproduce identically
  • Ubuntu 24.04, x86_64
  • Provider: custom OpenAI-compatible (@ai-sdk/openai-compatible) → llama.cpp llama-server, local 35B model, ~83 tok/s streaming
  • Driven over the REST API: POST /session → POST /session/{id}/prompt_async → poll GET /session/status

Reproduction

Attached script (zombie-test.sh) runs N control sessions then N trigger sessions, sequentially, 120s limit each.

Control prompt (never triggers):

Call the bash tool exactly once with the command: echo control-1.
Do not call any other tools. Then stop and give a one-word answer.

Trigger prompt (triggers every time):

In one single response, call the bash tool TWICE (two separate tool
calls in the same response): first command: mkdir -p /tmp/zt-1/a
second command: echo done-1. Then stop and give a one-word answer.

Results

version | control (1 call/turn) | trigger (2 calls/turn) -- | -- | -- v1.17.18 | 10/10 completed | 10/10 stuck busy forever v1.17.20 | 10/10 completed | 10/10 stuck busy forever

Every stuck session shows the identical fingerprint: message log contains 2 tool parts, exactly 1 frozen at "running", and /tmp/zt-N/a was never created on disk.

Evidence from a stuck session (original field incident)

Message log tail — mkdir "running" forever, sibling completed in 17ms:

json
{ "type": "tool", "tool": "bash",
  "state": { "status": "running",
    "input": { "command": "mkdir -p .../toolchains/android-sdk/cmdline-tools && mkdir -p .../toolchains/android-sdk" },
    "time": { "start": 1783992687894 } } }
{ "type": "tool", "tool": "bash",
  "state": { "status": "completed",
    "input": { "command": "which java && java -version 2>&1" },
    "metadata": { "exit": 1 },
    "time": { "start": 1783992688311, "end": 1783992688328 } } }

Provider-side timeline (llama-server logs): the model turn that emitted both calls finished generating normally; the provider was never asked for another turn afterward. While the session sat "busy" there were zero established sockets to the serve process and no child processes (verified with ss and ps) — nothing was executing, nothing was in flight.

Notes

  • Likely related (all report intermittent behavior with no deterministic repro): #22156, #27871, #14769, #20096, #6573.
  • Hypothesis for why this is deterministic here and intermittent elsewhere: the local provider streams at a very consistent rate (~83 tok/s, low jitter), so the two tool calls in a turn land at a highly repeatable interval — if the drop is a timing race in dispatch, a low-jitter emitter hits the vulnerable window every time, while cloud APIs (batched/jittery delivery) hit it occasionally.
  • Reviewed v1.17.19 and v1.17.20 release notes before filing: no changes to tool dispatch, session state, or serve mode, consistent with identical reproduction on both tested versions.

Happy to run patches or additional diagnostics against this setup — the repro takes ~10 minutes and triggers 100% of the time.

### Plugins None ### OpenCode version v1.17.18 and v1.17.20 — reproduced identically on both ### Steps to reproduce

  1. Run opencode serve with any OpenAI-compatible provider (reproduced with llama.cpp llama-server, local 35B model; low-jitter local providers trigger it 100%)

  2. Via REST: create a session (POST /session), then POST /session/{id}/prompt_async with:

    In one single response, call the bash tool TWICE (two separate tool
calls in the same response): first command: mkdir -p /tmp/zt-1/a
second command: echo done-1. Then stop and give a one-word answer.

  3. Poll GET /session/status — the session stays busy forever; the message log shows one tool part frozen at "running"; /tmp/zt-1/a is never created on disk.

Attached script automates 10 control + 10 trigger sessions:
zombie-test.sh

### Screenshot and/or share link Placeholder@Placeholder-PC:~/TheProject/window-temp-android$ export $(sudo cat /etc/opencode-serve.env) ~/zombie-test.sh === CONTROL arm (1 call/turn) === [control 1] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA [control 2] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA [control 3] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA [control 4] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA [control 5] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA [control 6] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA [control 7] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA [control 8] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA [control 9] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA [control 10] IDLE tools/running=1,0,ok dir=NA === TRIGGER arm (2 calls/turn) === [trigger 1] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO [trigger 2] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO [trigger 3] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO [trigger 4] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO [trigger 5] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO [trigger 6] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO [trigger 7] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO [trigger 8] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO [trigger 9] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO [trigger 10] STUCK tools/running=2,1,ok dir=NO === SUMMARY === 10 control IDLE 10 trigger STUCK full log: /home/complex/zombie-test-results.txt [zombie-test-results.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29992082/zombie-test-results.txt) ### Operating System Ubuntu 24.04 (x86_64) ### Terminal N/A — headless serve mode, driven via REST API (no TUI involved)

title: Add scrollbar support in question tool when option list is long
author: buguang01
body:
### Feature Description

When the `question` tool is used with a large number of options, the list extends beyond the visible terminal area and later options become inaccessible. It would be very helpful if the option list supported scrolling.

### Problem / Use Case

- In sub-agent workflows, the `question` tool may present many choices (e.g., configurations, parameters, or enumerated values).
- When the list is longer than the terminal height, the user cannot see or select the items that are rendered below the fold.
- This limits the usefulness of the tool in real-world scenarios with many options.

### Proposed Behavior

- If the number of options exceeds the available terminal display height, the `question` tool should render the list inside a scrollable view.
- The user should be able to scroll up/down to see all options and make a selection.
- Keyboard navigation (e.g., arrow keys, Page Up/Down) should work within the scrollable area.

### Alternatives

[If any workarounds exist, e.g., filtering options, paginating, describe them here]

### Screenshots

[Attach a screenshot showing the current behavior with many options cut off]

title: question tool fails to display options correctly in nested sub-agents
author: buguang01
body:
### Bug Description

When the OpenCode CLI spawns multiple layers of nested sub-agents, the `question` tool inside the sub-agents does not display options properly. As a result, interaction with the `question` tool becomes broken or unusable.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Start a task in the OpenCode CLI that uses sub-agents.
2. Let the sub-agent spawn further nested sub-agents.
3. Inside one of the nested sub-agents, the tool invokes the `question` tool to present options.
4. Observe that the options are not rendered correctly (details below).

### Expected Behavior

The `question` tool should render all options clearly and allow the user to select one, regardless of how many layers of sub-agents are active.

### Actual Behavior

The options from the `question` tool are not displayed correctly. [Please fill in: e.g., options are missing / text is garbled / the prompt does not appear / selection cannot be made]

### Environment

- OS: [Windows / macOS / Linux]
- OpenCode version: [e.g., v1.2.3]
- Terminal: [PowerShell / CMD / Bash / Zsh / etc.]
- Shell: [e.g., Git Bash, Windows Terminal, iTerm2]
- Reproducible: [Yes / No / Intermittent]

### Screenshots / Logs

[If applicable, add screenshots or terminal output here]
